  • Design AI-agent-powered intake flows that replace manual, multi-page credentialing forms with intelligent interactions. You'll define what agentic onboarding looks like at Headway — new interaction patterns, trust signals, and automation boundaries that don't yet exist in our design system.

  • Scale our self-serve onboarding vision beyond v2. We've proven that product-led onboarding can dramatically improve conversion; you'll push this further, enabling providers to go from interest to credentialed with minimal human intervention.

  • Start in planning mode, using tools like Claude.ai to clarify the problem space, understand data, and test initial assumptions. Prototype with Claude Code/Cursor and MagicPatterns to build and iterate on flows and IAs. Let AI generate the pixels and code while you define the right patterns, behaviors, and quality. Partner with product & Eng to decide when to move into production.

  • Conduct research and talk directly to providers to validate solutions, with the research team's support. Understand why providers get stuck, what builds their confidence, and where automation helps vs. hinders.

  • Contribute to the design system and culture, share your AI practice, prompts, and patterns with the design team.

You'll be a great fit if you:

  • Have 8+ years of product design experience, with significant time spent on onboarding, activation, or conversion-focused product work

  • Have shipped measurable conversion improvements — you think in funnels, know how to diagnose drop-off, and get excited when a design change moves a metric

  • Are a systems thinker who can reason about how a form redesign connects to CAQH, NPI, payer credentialing, and downstream provider activation — you design with awareness of the full pipeline

  • Have experience designing agentic or AI-powered workflows — conversational flows, intelligent automation, or replacing manual processes with AI-driven experiences (not just chatbots)

  • Bring strong visual and interaction design craft — you care deeply about the details and can produce polished, high-quality work at speed

  • Are comfortable with AI prototyping tools (Claude Code, Cursor, MagicPatterns) and see them as a core part of how you work

  • Want your work to make a meaningful difference in people's lives — this role directly impacts how many providers join Headway and how many patients get access to affordable mental healthcare

Compensation & Benefits:

The expected base pay range for this position is $212,000 - $265,000, based on a variety of factors including qualifications, experience, and geographic location. In addition to base salary, this role may be eligible for an equity grant, depending on the position and level.

We are committed to offering a comprehensive and competitive total rewards package, including robust health and wellness benefits, retirement savings, and meaningful ownership opportunities through equity. Compensation decisions are made holistically, ensuring fairness and alignment with market benchmarks while recognizing individual contributions and potential.

  • Benefits offered include:

    • Equity compensation

    • Medical, Dental, and Vision coverage

    • HSA / FSA

    • 401K

    • Work-from-Home Stipend

    • Therapy Reimbursement

    • 16-week parental leave for eligible employees

    • Carrot Fertility annual reimbursement and membership

    • 13 paid holidays each year as well as a Holiday Break during the week between December 25th and December 31st

    • Flexible PTO

    • Employee Assistance Program (EAP)

    • Training and professional development

We believe a team's strength is in its people, and we cannot achieve this mission without a team that reflects the diversity of this problem – across race, ethnicity, gender, sexuality, age, national origin, religion, family status, disability, military status, and experience. Headway is committed to the full inclusion of all qualified individuals. As part of this commitment, Headway will ensure that persons with disabilities are provided with reasonable accommodations. If reasonable accommodation is needed to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and/or receive other benefits and privileges of employment, please inform the recruiter when they contact you to schedule your interview.

Headway participates in E-Verify. To learn more, click here.

A notice to Headway applicants: To protect yourself against phishing and recruitment fraud, please note that Headway only accepts applications through our official careers page at https://headway.co/careers. Headway will never refer you to external websites, ask for payment or personal information, or conduct interviews via messaging apps. All official communication will come from a @findheadway.com email address. If you are contacted by someone claiming to be from Headway via an unofficial channel, please do not share any information and report it as spam.

hinge-health-senior-product-designer-edu.descriptionHtml:
About the Role

Hinge Health is a company that recognizes the value of design – as a Senior Product Designer, you’ll join a tight-knit, collaborative team of designers who care deeply about creating cohesive and intuitive product experiences. Our team is at the center of solving impactful problems for millions of members who are managing their joint and muscle health, and making a meaningful difference in their lives.

In this role, you are identifying the "right" problems to solve and bridging complex business goals with user needs. You will own a broad product area within your pod and lead the end-to-end design process—from initial discovery and validated hypothesis testing to final high-fidelity execution and engineering handoff. You are expected to proactively reduce ambiguity and ensure that even the most complex user flows feel intuitive, contemporary, and simple.

We’re looking for designers that excel in the ability to simplify complex challenges into impactful user experiences, care deeply about the quality of their work (the "last 10%" of polish), and bring a genuine desire to learn about our members.

What You'll Accomplish

  • End-to-End Delivery: Deliver pod-level business outcomes by managing high-complexity projects from concept to launch.

  • Hypothesis-Driven Design: Turn ambiguous business goals into a roadmap of validated hypotheses and define success through measurable user outcomes.

  • Collaborate Across Teams: Work with cross-functional teams to create an integrated experience across mobile and desktop platforms.

  • Optimization: Use A/B testing, user research, and data science insights to refine product strategies, improving time-to-value and member engagement.

  • Design at Scale: Use and contribute to our design system to build scalable components and patterns that support consistency and efficiency.

  • Champion Quality: Obsess over micro-interactions, edge cases, and visual polish to raise the perceived quality of your pod's output.

  • Mentorship: Act as a mentor and role model for less experienced designers, providing direct, actionable feedback to elevate team excellence.

Basic Qualifications

  • A minimum 2+ years of product design experience, including partnering with product and engineering teams.

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Interaction, Industrial, Product, Service design, HCI, or equivalent experience.

  • A polished portfolio demonstrating end-to-end ownership of successful product launches, with strengths in both interaction and visual design.

  • Command of visual design principles (layout, iconography, typography) with a track record of advancing interface aesthetics.

  • Fidelity for resonance: Proficient in prototyping across multiple fidelities to test emotional resonance and communicate design intent.

  • Data fluency: Comfortable using research and data to shape, validate, and articulate design decisions.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Design thinker for complex systems: Energized by thorny user needs and opportunities to simplify workflow inefficiencies.

  • AI Curiosity: Actively explore how emerging tools like generative AI can enhance workflows or define the logic for AI-driven features (addressing trust and transparency).

  • Multimedia Mastery: Experience designing seamlessly across mobile, desktop, and wearables.

  • Passion for Healthcare: Committed to helping others live healthier lives through design and technology, with relevant industry experience.


Beware of Phishing Attempts: We've noticed an increase in phishing where fraudsters impersonate employees and send fake job offers to steal sensitive information. We'll never ask for financial details during the hiring process and only use "@hingehealth.com" emails. If you receive a suspicious offer, stop communication and report it to the US FBI Internet Crime Complaint Center. To verify an email from our recruiting team, forward it to security@hingehealth.com.

hinge-health-senior-product-designer-new.descriptionHtml:
About the Role

In this role, you’ll help shape and launch new digital care program experiences as Hinge Health expands into new condition categories and explores net-new member journeys. You’ll work from ambiguous problem spaces to define what should exist, validate the opportunity, and design thoughtful end-to-end experiences that can succeed in the market.

As a Senior Product Designer, you’ll collaborate closely with product, engineering, clinical, data, and commercial partners to create intuitive, user-centered solutions from concept to execution, including discovery work, interaction and visual design, prototyping, and validation.

We’re looking for a designer who excels at simplifying complex challenges into impactful user experiences, cares deeply about craft and quality, has a strong track record of shipping meaningful work, brings a growth mindset, and is energized by learning quickly in unfamiliar domains.

Hinge Health is scaling with 51% year-over-year revenue gains and a member base expanding by over 47% annually. This role can directly power how we bring new programs to life and how members experience Hinge Health as we grow.

Learn more about Design at Hinge Health.

What You’ll Accomplish

  • Shape 0→1 program experiences by turning ambiguous opportunities into clear, compelling product directions and tangible member experiences.

  • Lead discovery and validation work to identify unmet member needs, prototype lean solutions, and know when there is enough signal to move forward or change direction.

  • Design high-quality end-to-end flows for new programs, from first concepts through launch, with strong attention to usability, clarity, and emotional experience.

  • Partner across product, engineering, clinical, data, and commercial teams to move priorities forward and build alignment without relying on formal authority.

  • Create structure in unfamiliar domains by synthesizing user needs, clinical context, business goals, and technical constraints into clear hypotheses and design decisions.

  • Contribute scalable patterns and design principles that help new programs feel cohesive within the broader Hinge Health ecosystem.

  • Champion quality, accessibility, and inclusivity so new experiences are thoughtful and effective for a diverse range of members.

Basic Qualifications

  • 3+ years of product design experience, including close partnership with product and engineering teams.

  • Bachelor’s degree in Interaction, Industrial, Product, or Service Design, Human-Computer Interaction, or equivalent practical experience.

  • Experience leading ambiguous problem spaces and translating business goals into clear user problems, testable hypotheses, and effective product directions.

  • Strong interaction and visual design skills, with a track record of simplifying complex flows into polished, intuitive experiences.

  • Proven ability to drive end-to-end execution on complex initiatives, anticipate risks, and deliver work with clarity and reliability.

  • Strong communication and influence skills, including the ability to align cross-functional partners and advocate for a clear point of view with senior stakeholders.

  • Experience using prototypes, qualitative feedback, and quantitative signals to inform design decisions and improve outcomes.

Preferred Qualifications

  • 5+ years of product design experience, including close partnership with product and engineering teams

  • A standout and up-to-date portfolio demonstrating high-impact product work, strong craft, and measurable outcomes.
    Experience designing 0→1 products, new program launches, or other greenfield consumer experiences.

  • Experience in digital health, wellness, or other clinically informed consumer product environments.

  • Comfort balancing direct user needs with business and commercial priorities, and making thoughtful tradeoffs across both.

  • Experience working across complex service journeys that involve multiple stakeholders, workflows, or care touchpoints.

  • Strong facilitation and storytelling skills that help teams align on decisions, principles, and next steps.

  • Curiosity about emerging tools, including AI-assisted workflows, to improve speed, quality, and learning.

  • Passion for helping people live healthier lives through thoughtful, human-centered design.

About Hinge Health

Hinge Health uses software, including AI, to help automate care for joint and muscle health, improve member outcomes, and reduce costs for clients. The platform supports a wide range of Musculoskeletal care needs, from acute injury and chronic pain to post-surgical rehabilitation, and helps members improve pain and function while accessing care from anywhere.

Hinge Health is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

Hinge Health Hybrid Model (SF-Based)

We believe remote and in-person work each have real strengths, and we aim to get the best of both. Employees in hybrid roles are expected to work from the San Francisco office 3 days per week for the full 8 hours of a typical business day. The San Francisco office is also dog-friendly.

What You'll Love About Us

  • Participation in our Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP), allowing you to acquire company stock via payroll at a 15% discount against the lower market price.

  • Comprehensive and inclusive medical coverage and benefit programs for you and your dependents.

  • Flexible retirement savings through Traditional or Roth 401(k) investment paths.

  • Dedicated lifestyle stipends designed to fuel your professional growth and continuous learning.

Culture and Engagement

Hinge Health is an equal opportunity employer and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind. We consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal history, consistent with legal requirements, and provide reasonable accommodations for candidates with disabilities.

By submitting an application, candidates acknowledge that their personal data will be handled according to the personnel and candidate privacy policy.


Beware of Phishing Attempts: We've noticed an increase in phishing where fraudsters impersonate employees and send fake job offers to steal sensitive information. We'll never ask for financial details during the hiring process and only use "@hingehealth.com" emails. If you receive a suspicious offer, stop communication and report it to the US FBI Internet Crime Complaint Center. To verify an email from our recruiting team, forward it to security@hingehealth.com.

hinge-health-staff-product-designer-desi.descriptionHtml:
About the Role

Hinge Health is a company that recognizes the value of design – as a Staff Product Designer on our Design System team, you’ll join a tight-knit, collaborative team who cares deeply about creating cohesive and intuitive product experiences. Our team is at the center of solving impactful problems for millions of members who are managing their joint and muscle health, and making a meaningful difference in their lives.

In this role, you’ll directly shape the future of our Design System, focused on creating and evolving shared patterns and components to ensure a cohesive user experience across Hinge Health’s product suite. You’ll define not just what the system is, but how it evolves, how decisions get made, and how consistency and flexibility are balanced as Hinge Health ships rapidly.

This is a high-impact role, at an especially exciting time in Hinge Health’s journey. As we scale with 51% year-over-year revenue gains and a member base expanding by over 47% annually, this role will be instrumental in sustaining our momentum and shaping the future of care.

Learn more about Design at Hinge Health.

What You'll Accomplish

Own the Design System

  • Lead the vision, strategy, and roadmap for Hinge Health’s design system.

  • Define and evolve core components, patterns, tokens, and interaction standards used across the product.

  • Ensure the system supports a wide range of surfaces, from core product UI to Agent flows, enterprise templates, and integrated design systems.

Partner Deeply with Engineering

  • Collaborate closely with engineers on implementation details, tradeoffs, and system architecture.

  • Influence code-level decisions that affect system consistency and adoption.

  • Help shape tooling, Storybook usage, and contribution workflows so the system is easy to use and maintain.

Set Standards & Governance

  • Partner with feature teams through office hours and design reviews. You will help teams navigate patterns the system doesn't yet cover and decide when a design should become a global standard or remain a local solution.

  • Establish clear contribution models, review processes, and decision-making frameworks for system changes.

  • Act as a design system “domain owner,” providing guidance and sign-off on system-level decisions.

  • Document principles, patterns, and rationale so decisions scale beyond individuals.

Raise the Bar for Design Quality

  • Identify inconsistencies, duplication, and fragmentation across the product and drive systematic improvements.

  • Balance speed with craft, knowing when to be opinionated and when to enable flexibility.

  • Advocate for system-level thinking across product, design, and engineering.

Basic Qualifications

  • 2+ years of product design experience, with deep ownership of a design system at scale.

  • A standout portfolio showcasing high-impact design systems work and leadership across complex systems initiatives.

  • Highly effective communicator with the ability to influence senior stakeholders and align teams around design direction.

  • Demonstrated success mentoring designers and elevating the quality of team output.

  • Deep fluency with tokens, component architecture, variants, and Figma systems craft.

  • Experience with Claude Code (or other agentic coding tools), MCPs, and other AI workflows.

  • Strong opinions on system governance, contribution models, and adoption strategies.

  • Experience partnering with platform engineers on implementation, parity, and release cadence.

About Hinge Health

Hinge Health leverages software, including AI, to largely automate care for joint and muscle health, delivering an outstanding member experience, improved member outcomes, and cost reductions for its clients. The company has designed its platform to address a broad spectrum of MSK care—from acute injury, to chronic pain, to post-surgical rehabilitation—and the platform can help to ease members’ pain, improve their function, and reduce their need for surgeries, all while driving health equity by allowing members to engage in their exercise therapy sessions from anywhere. The company is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

Hinge Health Hybrid Model

We believe remote and in-person work each have real strengths, and we aim to get the best of both. Employees in hybrid roles are expected to work from the San Francisco office 3 days per week for the full 8 hours of a typical business day.

What You'll Love About Us

  • Participation in our Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP), allowing you to acquire company stock via payroll at a 15% discount against the lower market price.

  • Comprehensive and inclusive medical coverage and benefit programs for you and your dependents.

  • Flexible retirement savings through Traditional or Roth 401(k) investment paths.

  • Dedicated lifestyle stipends designed to fuel your professional growth and continuous learning.

Culture and Engagement

Hinge Health is an equal opportunity employer and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind. We consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal history, consistent with legal requirements, and provide reasonable accommodations for candidates with disabilities. By submitting an application, candidates acknowledge that their personal data will be handled according to the personnel and candidate privacy policy.


Beware of Phishing Attempts: We've noticed an increase in phishing where fraudsters impersonate employees and send fake job offers to steal sensitive information. We'll never ask for financial details during the hiring process and only use "@hingehealth.com" emails. If you receive a suspicious offer, stop communication and report it to the US FBI Internet Crime Complaint Center. To verify an email from our recruiting team, forward it to security@hingehealth.com.

hinge-health-staff-product-designer-outc.descriptionHtml:
About the Role

Hinge Health values design as a strategic partner in shaping product direction and member experience. As a Staff Product Designer on the Outcomes Pod, you will focus on helping people understand, log, and act on their progress over time. At the Staff level, you will lead large ambiguous problem spaces, shape product vision and strategy with partners, and align senior leaders around a clear design point of view. You will partner closely with Product, Engineering, Data, and other cross-functional teammates to turn complex, data-rich, emotionally sensitive moments into simple and motivating experiences. We are looking for a designer with strong UI/UX craft, deep product thinking, and the ability to simplify complex clinical data into comprehensible member-facing insights. This is a high-impact role for someone who wants to shape how Hinge Health helps members see progress, stay engaged, and build confidence in their care journey. Hinge Health is building a new healthcare system, one built around the person, with a mission to revolutionize the treatment and prevention of pain and help move people beyond pain. This position provides a unique opportunity to drive high-impact outcomes during a transformative phase of growth. As we scale with 51% year-over-year revenue gains and a member base expanding by over 47% annually, your leadership will be instrumental in sustaining our momentum and shaping the future of digital care.

What You'll Accomplish

  • Lead the design vision and strategy for the Outcomes pod’s member-facing experiences, with primary focus on the Progress tab, pain logging, and the broader story members see about their improvement over time.

  • Improve how and when members share pain data and create useful feedback loops.

  • Translate complex clinical and behavioral data into simple, motivating interfaces that help members understand trends, next best actions, and the value of staying engaged.

  • Partner with Product, Engineering, and Data to define hypotheses, run experiments, and use qualitative and quantitative signals to improve member experience and outcomes.

  • Raise the quality bar across the pod by creating frameworks, patterns, and design principles that make the work more coherent and scalable over time.

  • Influence adjacent teams when shared surfaces, shared metrics, or shared behaviors affect outcomes-related experiences.

  • Champion accessibility, clarity, and product quality so members with different needs, contexts, and motivation levels can succeed.

Basic Qualifications

  • Minimum 3+ years of product design experience, including close partnership with product and engineering teams.

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Interaction, Industrial, Product, or Service Design,

    Human-Computer Interaction, or equivalent experience.

  • Expertise in interaction and visual design, with a track record of advancing craft quality across teams.

  • Experience leading ambiguous problem spaces, setting vision, and defining design strategy across products or platforms.

  • Strong fluency in data-informed design, including forming hypotheses, partnering on experiments, facilitating research, and translating insights into action.

  • Excellent communication and influence skills, with the ability to align cross-functional partners and senior stakeholders around design direction.

  • Demonstrated success mentoring other designers and elevating the quality and impact of team output.

  • Strong prototyping and systems-thinking skills, with the ability to create cohesive experiences across flows, components, and product surfaces.

Preferred Qualifications

  • 3+ years of product design experience, including close partnership with product and engineering teams.

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Interaction, Industrial, Product, or Service Design

  • Experience designing progress tracking, dashboards, data visualization, or other data-rich product experiences.

  • Experience simplifying complex systems into intuitive consumer-facing experiences, especially in health, wellness, or habit-forming product spaces.

  • Proven facilitation skills and comfort leading leadership conversations on large,ambiguous, cross-functional initiatives.

  • Experience contributing to or shaping design systems that scale quality and consistency across products.

  • Curiosity about emerging AI tools and workflows, and thoughtful judgment about where they can improve design quality or speed.

  • Passion for helping people live healthier lives through thoughtful design and technology.

About Hinge Health


Hinge Health leverages software, including AI, to largely automate care for joint and muscle health, delivering an outstanding member experience, improved member outcomes, and cost reductions for its clients. The company has designed its platform to address a broad spectrum of MSK care—from acute injury, to chronic pain, to post-surgical rehabilitation—and the platform can help to ease members’ pain, improve their function, and reduce their need for surgeries, all while driving health equity by allowing members to engage in their exercise therapy sessions from anywhere. The company is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

Learn more athttp://www.hingehealth.com

What You'll Love About Us

  • Participation in our Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP), allowing you to acquire company stock via payroll at a 15% discount against the lower market price.

  • Comprehensive and inclusive medical coverage and benefit programs for you and your dependents.

  • Flexible retirement savings through Traditional or Roth 401(k) investment paths.

  • Dedicated lifestyle stipends designed to fuel your professional growth and continuous learning.

Culture and Engagement

Hinge Health is an equal opportunity employer and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind. We make employment decisions without regards to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, veteran status, disability status, pregnancy, or any other basis protected by federal, state or local law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. We provide reasonable accommodations for candidates with disabilities. If you feel you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, let us know by reaching out to your recruiter. By submitting your application you are acknowledging we are using your personal data as outlined in the personnel and candidate privacy policy.


Beware of Phishing Attempts: We've noticed an increase in phishing where fraudsters impersonate employees and send fake job offers to steal sensitive information. We'll never ask for financial details during the hiring process and only use "@hingehealth.com" emails. If you receive a suspicious offer, stop communication and report it to the US FBI Internet Crime Complaint Center. To verify an email from our recruiting team, forward it to security@hingehealth.com.

hone-health-product-designer-products-in.descriptionHtml:
About Hone

Hone is an online medical clinic at the forefront of transforming healthcare and enhancing longevity. We use cutting-edge scientific advancements to empower men and women to take control of their health and unlock their full potential. Our people are the heart of everything we do and drive our success. We approach every project through our brand values:

  • Fight for the Customer
  • Execute Ruthlessly
  • Communicate Candidly, Clearly, and Kindly
  • Collaborate Selflessly
  • Practice Calculated Risk-Taking
  • Maximize Joy and Gratitude

Hone has been fully virtual from day one and will continue to be a remote-first employer.

Our Ideal Candidate

Our ideal candidate is a mission-driven, motivated multi-tasker who is invested in work that is fulfilling and impactful. They embrace change and tackle challenges with enthusiasm. They have an “all-in” disposition towards work, understanding that we are a fast-paced, high-growth organization with evolving priorities. They can excel at both independent tasks and collaborative work, leading with clear and candid communication. They exhibit humble leadership—the ability to drive initiatives forward while remaining excited about continuous learning and development opportunities. They feel strongly about being part of a team that advocates for people to live longer and better lives.

The Role
We’re looking for a Product Designer to join our Products & Integrations team. In this role, you’ll work as a critical contributor to own the end-to-end experience of discovering, researching, purchasing, and completing additional diagnostics and treatments. You’ll report directly to the Head of Product Design and partner with our design, product management, engineering, clinical, and marketing teams to improve this experience and subsequent patient outcomes.

You’ll thrive in this role if you:

  • Have experience designing world-class e-commerce and marketing experiences
  • Have a strong eye for design and care deeply about craft
  • Excel at crafting bespoke marketing assets (product images, renderings, graphics, etc.)
  • Enjoy blending strategic thinking and tactical execution, getting into the nitty details around user flows, screens, and UI states.
  • Thinks broadly and explores multiple options before honing in on a final solution
  • Enjoys collaborating with cross-functional team members – Product Managers, Engineers, and other Designers
  • Can create world-class progressive designs that balance innovation, usability, and accessibility
  • Takes pride in translating complex information into intuitive, elegant, and delightful interfaces
  • Have a personal interest in preventative health and longevity

Primary ResponsibilitiesKey responsibilities for this role include (but are not limited to) the following:

  • Work strategically as a partner with product management, engineering, and design leadership to define and design the product experience within our patient portal/application.
  • Think big and explore divergent concepts while understanding how to converge and build iteratively towards your vision.
  • Work across the design process, from user flows and low-fidelity wireframes to high-fidelity mockups and interactive prototypes to explore and communicate ideas.
  • Create outstanding marketing assets (custom images, iconography, etc.) that elevate the design of the page and connect with the intended audience.
  • Provide creative and art direction to external partners (photographers, videographers, etc.)
  • Advocate for design by sharing your work and presenting cross-functionally to senior stakeholders/leadership. Be able to articulate design decisions and goals.
  • Participate in workshops, brainstorming sessions, and design thinking exercises.

Qualifications
To qualify for this role, candidates must meet the following criteria:

  • 4+ years of experience in consumer product design, working as part of a product team in an agile environment
  • A portfolio of outstanding design for consumer products, ecommerce, and marketing websites that showcase attention to detail and strong foundations in typography, interaction, visual design, and storytelling
  • Highly proficient with current design and prototyping tools (Figma, Photoshop, etc.)
  • Strong visual design craft
  • Excellent communication skills

Compensation Range
$130,000 - $150,000 based on location and experience

Benefits*

Hone wants our team to be in the best condition of their lives, so we offer a range of benefits including:

  • A remote-first work environment
  • Competitive compensation and equity options
  • Health, dental, and vision insurance coverage
  • Short-term disability and basic life coverage
  • Flexible Spending Accounts (FSAs)
  • Lifestyle Spending Accounts (LSAs)
  • We follow federal holidays and have uncapped time off for exempt employees
  • Budget for the technology tools you need (laptop, monitor, and/or special software)
  • A focus on company-sponsored activities to foster engagement (both virtual and in-person)
  • Waived membership fees for any Hone team members utilizing Hone products

*These benefits are available to full-time, regular employees, and not to independent contractors, part-time employees, temporary employees, or interns.

We are proud to be an equal-opportunity workplace committed to building a team culture that celebrates diversity and inclusion. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions. Please contact us to request accommodation.

About Hone

Hone is an online medical clinic at the forefront of transforming healthcare and enhancing longevity. We use cutting-edge scientific advancements to empower men and women to take control of their health and unlock their full potential. Our ideal candidate is a mission-driven, motivated multi-tasker who is invested in work that is fulfilling and impactful. They embrace change and tackle challenges with enthusiasm. They have an “all-in” disposition towards work, understanding that we are a fast-paced, high-growth organization with evolving priorities. The Clinical Experience team is focused on building a world-class longevity platform that aids our doctors in providing the best medical care. You’ll work cross-functionally across the full product development lifecycle to concept, test, and refine solutions.

  • Lead workshops, brainstorming sessions, and design thinking exercises.
  • Help shape the culture of a growing design team.
  • Help lead innovation across the company by driving the adoption of user-centered processes, methodologies, and tools.

Qualifications
