whoop-senior-product-designer-growth.descriptionHtml:
At WHOOP, we're on a mission to unlock human performance and Healthspan. WHOOP empowers members to perform at a higher level through a deeper understanding of their bodies and daily lives. Our wearable technology and analytics platform help individuals optimize recovery, training, and overall health.

WHOOP is seeking a Senior Product Designer to join the Growth team. You’ll focus on crafting world-class digital experiences that drive growth outcomes like acquisition, conversion, and engagement, with an emphasis on ecommerce and core purchase journeys. We’re looking for a designer with strong visual fundamentals and a sharp sense for how people make decisions in complex, multi-step flows, someone who can bring clarity to complexity, think in funnels, and use experimentation to continuously improve the experience.

RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Own and lead the end-to-end design of critical digital experiences that drive acquisition, conversion, and retention, with a focus on ecommerce experiences.

  • Shape and evolve complex user flows and information architecture to create clear, intuitive paths to conversion as our offerings evolve.

  • Balance near-term execution with long-term thinking by designing improvements that ladder into an evolving platform architecture, ensuring today’s decisions set up scalable, cohesive experiences over time.

  • Translate growth opportunities into thoughtful product solutions grounded in user behavior, data insights, and effective design patterns, consistently delivering work with exceptional visual craft.

  • Work closely with product managers, engineers, and data analysts to shape strategy and drive execution, ensuring high-quality implementation and moving initiatives forward with clarity.

  • Work within an experimentation-driven team, using data to inform hypotheses, design A/B tests, and iterate quickly while driving measurable improvements in key conversion metrics and balancing speed and craft.

  • Take ownership of key initiatives by defining problems, shaping direction, and driving work forward with a clear point of view grounded in strong design fundamentals.

  • Leverage AI and emerging tools to accelerate exploration, prototyping, and iteration, bringing a technically curious mindset and comfort working close to implementation.

  • Contribute to WHOOP’s design culture by sharing expertise, giving feedback, and helping raise the bar for product quality across the team.

QUALIFICATIONS:

  • Multiple years of experience designing ecommerce, purchase flows, or other transaction-oriented products, with a deep understanding of how users evaluate, decide, and complete actions within digital experiences (not including co-ops, internships, or academic projects).

  • An exceptional portfolio of shipped digital product work that demonstrates exceptional visual craft, clarity in complex flows, and the ability to deliver production-ready experiences.

  • Proven experience designing for measurable, conversion-focused outcomes such as acquisition, purchase, or retention, with a strong understanding of funnel optimization, experimentation, and using data to inform and evaluate decisions.

  • Demonstrated strength in information architecture and designing end-to-end flows that simplify complexity and guide users through multi-step journeys.

  • Deep proficiency with modern design tools and workflows (Figma, prototyping, component libraries), combined with strong technical fluency and the ability to leverage AI and code-adjacent tools to accelerate design and prototyping, while collaborating closely with engineering to bring ideas to life.

  • Strong ability to own and drive multiple initiatives simultaneously, influencing cross-functional partners and influencing product direction while elevating team craft.

  • Clear decision-maker and communicator who can articulate and defend design decisions using strong design principles, heuristics, and data, building alignment across stakeholders.

This role is based in the WHOOP office located in Boston, MA. The successful candidate must be prepared to relocate if necessary to work out of the Boston, MA office.

yarn-founding-product-designer.descriptionHtml:
Video is the most compelling medium ever invented.

But even a single minute of video can take hours to make and expertise across multiple tools.

AI is changing this forever, and how we create and consume video will look completely different in 2 or 3 years time.

We’re building a creative tool for content engineers (think Cursor) along with the agentic infrastructure to scale that content to infinity (think Devin).

We’re a small experienced team based in Soho NYC.

Our investors include YCombinator, General Catalyst, Leonis Capital, Race Capital, and angels from Pika Labs, Meta, Roblox, and Adobe.

“Yarn has changed how we think about video. We’re now able to make high-retention content at scale – it feels like a secret weapon. I haven’t seen anyone else doing video like this with AI.” Varun Anand, Cofounder @ Clay

About the role

We’re building in stealth with world-class teams at companies like Clay and Shopify, and we’re looking for a founding product design role in-person in NYC.

We’ve hit an inflection point on fit with our customers and now looking to aggressively grow the team across design, engineering, and AI.

We looking for our first full-time product designer. We’re design-led and already have a strong visual designer and FE-eng capabilities in house – you will be part of a growing design team. We take design extremely seriously.

Someone with an extremely good eye, the ability to reason and think creatively, able to push the boundaries of what’s possible, and have a founder mindset with strong equity.

Things you might work on immediately

  • Design core agent interfaces and workflows.
  • Prototype new creative tool experiences using AI.

    You might also
  • Help spec and design new motion graphics templates and models.
  • Design and write WebGL shaders for special FX.
  • Jump into CSS or micro-interactions to up the polish and delight in the product.
  • Collaborate with LLM engineers on building design and taste-heavy AI experiences.

How we work

  • We believe in small, hard-working, talent-dense teams.
  • We deeply care about our customers and relentlessly iterate and collaborate with them.
  • We're generalists that enjoy picking up new challenges and learning new tech.
  • We prototype broadly across technologies and UXes, then make aggressive product plays.

zello-product-designer-design-systems-co.descriptionHtml:
IMPORTANT: Please be aware, scammers may try to impersonate Zello by reaching out regarding job opportunities. We will never ask you for bank account information, checks, or other sensitive information as part of our hiring process. All correspondence will come from the zello.com email domain. If you’re unsure, please email recruiting@zello.com with questions.

About Zello

Zello is a voice-first communication platform, powered by our industry-leading push-to-talk technology, to improve collaboration and productivity for desk-less workers. With over 175+ million users, we’re the #1 rated push-to-talk app in the world, delivering 9 billion (yes, with a B) messages a month.

At Zello, our company values are at the heart of what we do everyday. We’re proud to serve the frontline, we’re privileged to connect people in times of crisis across the globe, and we’re honored to support first responders.

And this is where you come in.

Zello's design system supports iOS, Android, Desktop, and Hardware products — and it's being asked to do more. As the system expands across three distinct platform paradigms simultaneously, design needs a senior partner who can take architectural ownership of the backlog, drive cross-platform consistency decisions, and build direct working relationships with iOS, Android, and React engineering leads. This contractor brings IC-4 platform authority from day one, with the opportunity to convert to a full-time role based on performance across both DS and product design work.

About Zello

Zello is the leading push-to-talk communication platform, enabling instant voice communication for frontline workers across industries like logistics, hospitality, transportation, and construction. We build for the people who keep the world running — the warehouse supervisor coordinating a crew, the flight crew managing a gate turnover, the field technician working in a loud environment where clarity and speed matter. Our product has to work the first time, every time.

A mature and growing design system is what allows us to keep pace with the product's ambition. This role is how we get there.

The Role

This is a 6-month contract engagement (3-month minimum) reporting to the Manager of Product Design. You'll partner closely with our Staff Product Designer as the primary architectural counterpart for DS work across iOS, Android, and React. Day-to-day execution and prioritization within initiative scope is self-managed — Design Leadership sets direction at the initiative level; you own the work from there. As DS work matures in months 2–3 (possibly shorter), you'll take on product design projects as a full contributing member of the team.

After a Successful First 3 Months, You Will Have

  • Delivered a written gap assessment and prioritized DS backlog by end of week 2, with priorities ranked across iOS, Android, and React and rationale documented — giving the team a clear, current picture of what the system needs.

  • Shipped production-quality Figma components for iOS (UIKit/SwiftUI) and Android (Material Design 3) that passed engineer review without structural rework — not once, but as a consistent baseline.

  • Owned the React token pipeline end-to-end — Style Dictionary configuration current, Storybook documentation complete and usable by engineers and designers without a walkthrough.

  • Completed a cross-platform consistency audit — shared conventions documented, conflicts resolved, and platform-specific exceptions justified in writing so the next person doesn't have to reverse-engineer the decisions.

  • Contributed product design work — screens, flows, and features — at IC-4 quality as DS capacity opened up, with output that reflected independent platform judgment and required minimal rework.

What You'll Do

  • Take architectural ownership of DS work across iOS, Android, and React — make decisions, document tradeoffs, and maintain a visible, current view of the backlog in Jira without needing prompting.

  • Build and ship production-quality Figma components for iOS (UIKit/SwiftUI) and Android (Material Design 3) with genuine platform fidelity — behavioral accuracy, not just visual resemblance.

  • Own the React token pipeline end-to-end: Style Dictionary configuration, Storybook documentation, and engineering handoff quality.

  • Drive cross-platform consistency audits — identify gaps, resolve conflicts across platforms, and document shared conventions that outlast the engagement.

  • Partner directly with iOS, Android, and React engineering leads to validate that Figma components map correctly to production implementations — surfacing discrepancies before they ship, not after.

  • Define component documentation standards and write usage guidelines that engineers and designers can apply without additional walkthroughs.

  • Identify systemic issues proactively — missing states, undocumented edge cases, token inconsistencies — and bring recommendations rather than flags.

  • Contribute to product design work — screens, flows, and feature UX — as DS work matures (expect this to begin in earnest by months 2–3).

Who You Are

  • You've built native iOS components with genuine UIKit/SwiftUI behavioral fidelity — not just visual resemblance to iOS patterns — and you can walk through the platform-specific decisions in your portfolio without notes.

  • You've built Android components that are genuinely distinct from your iOS equivalents, reflecting Material Design 3 conventions, and you can discuss the Compose vs. View-based component model with an Android engineer.

  • You've owned a React token pipeline end-to-end — architecture, Style Dictionary configuration, and the tradeoffs you made — and you can explain every decision and what you'd do differently.

  • You've written Storybook documentation, not just referenced it, and you have examples of component docs that others have used without needing a walkthrough from you.

  • You've held engineering leads accountable to design fidelity — you can describe a specific time you surfaced a design-to-code discrepancy before it shipped and what you did about it.

  • You've managed your own DS backlog with minimal direction. You didn't wait to be told what mattered — you identified systemic issues, prioritized them, and communicated status proactively.

  • You have product design work in your portfolio alongside DS artifacts — screens, flows, or features — and your approach reflects platform-appropriate judgment, not just execution of a brief.

  • 7+ years of experience; IC-4 platform depth across iOS, Android, and React is the bar.

Compensation

$105/hour · Up to 40 hours/week · 6-month contract strongly preferred (3-month minimum).

Conversion to full-time is possible for contractors who perform at a high level across both DS and product design work over the engagement. This is an opportunity to earn — not an expected outcome — and will be evaluated at the end of the contract period.

We hire for potential, passion for our mission, and a knack for solving difficult problems over checking every qualification box. We have competitive pay, equity with significant upside, and intentionally design our benefits to encourage healthy and well-balanced employees, flexible schedules and time off. We even offer a sabbatical after every five years of service so you’re able to pursue and enjoy what matters most to you. And of course, we wouldn’t be a technology company without a ping-pong table and free snacks in our break room. Join us!

Zello provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws.

All Zello personnel are required to comply with defined security, privacy, and compliance requirements applicable to their role along with requirements that are applicable to all Zello personnel.
